description_long: This object is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a domed, slightly flattened profile. Its surface is predominantly gray with subtle gradients of lighter and darker tones, and it features natural, undulating contours that suggest weathering and age. The stone has a matte, rough finish, with visible striations and mottling that give it a realistic, organic appearance. One side of the stone is marked with black alphanumeric text, likely for cataloging or identification purposes. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one edge appearing slightly more angular and the opposite side more rounded. The stone's texture and coloration suggest it could be found in urban landscaping or as a natural obstacle in parks or sidewalks.
description: A medium-sized, gray, dome-shaped stone with rough, mottled surface and visible cataloging text on one side.
description_view_0: The stone appears domed and rounded, with its broadest, smoothest surface visible and no text apparent.
description_view_1: This view shows the side with black alphanumeric text, highlighting a slightly more angular edge and the stone's layered texture.
description_view_2: The stone's profile is more angular and the text is partially visible, emphasizing the irregular, natural contours.
description_view_3: A rounded, smooth side of the stone is visible, with no text and a more uniform gray coloration.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and striations with irregular, natural veining; one side has artificial black text marking.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and matte, with visible signs of natural weathering and minor erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones; may contrast with paved surfaces or manicured lawns.
